Meta / CLI

Meta CLI

Core metadata command-line tools.

Command-line tools

Select an item from the navigation to open its reference page.

CLISummary
meta `meta` is the core workspace CLI for creating, inspecting, editing, comparing, and maintaining modeled metadata.
meta-docs `meta-docs` turns CLI definitions, workspace models, selected instance facts, and authored narrative into refreshable reference documentation.
meta-weave `meta-weave` creates MetaWeave workspaces for explicit model-to-model weaving, where structure is carried across modeled surfaces instead of hidden in ad hoc conversion code.

Meta / CLI

meta

`meta` is the core workspace CLI for creating, inspecting, editing, comparing, and maintaining modeled metadata.

Commands

32 commands exposed by meta.

CommandSummary
meta bulk-insert Bulk insert instances from tsv/csv input. Use --auto-id only for new rows whose source data does not carry an external Id.
meta delete Delete one instance by Id.
meta insert Insert one instance by explicit Id or auto-generated numeric Id. Use --auto-id only when creating a brand-new row with no external identity.
meta instance Diff/merge instances and apply instance updates.
meta query Search instances using equals/contains filters.
meta check Run model + instance integrity checks.
meta graph Inspect relationship graph structure.
meta list List entities, properties, or relationships.
meta model Inspect and edit model entities, properties, and relationships.
meta model add-entity Add a new entity definition.
meta model rename-model Rename the model contract and instance root element.
meta model rename-entity Atomically rename an entity, update relationship targets, and rename implied non-role relationship fields.
meta model drop-entity Drop an entity (blocked if instances or inbound relationships exist).
meta model add-property Add an entity property.
meta model set-property-required Change whether a property is required; use --default-value to backfill existing rows when making a property required.
meta model rename-property Rename a property in one entity.
meta model drop-property Drop a property from an entity.
meta model rename-relationship Rename a relationship usage by setting or clearing its role; instance relationship field names are rewritten atomically.
meta model add-relationship Add a required relationship; use --default-id to backfill existing source instances.
meta model refactor Run atomic model+instance refactors.
meta model refactor property-to-relationship Atomically convert a scalar source property to a required relationship using a target lookup key.
meta model refactor relationship-to-property Atomically convert a required relationship back to a required scalar Id property.
meta model drop-relationship Drop a declared relationship (blocked if in use).
meta model suggest Read-only relationship inference from model + instance data. Strong suggestions require one exact eligible Id-based target; weak suggestions cover role-style suffix matches and other ambiguous multi-target cases.
meta view View entity schema or one instance.
meta deploy Deploy generated artifacts to external targets.
meta export Export workspace data to external formats.
meta generate Generate artifacts from the workspace.
meta import Import from SQL into a new workspace, or import CSV into new/existing workspace.
meta init Initialize a metadata workspace.
meta status Show workspace summary and model/instance sizes.
meta workspace Workspace-level operations.
meta bulk-insert Bulk insert instances from tsv/csv input. Use --auto-id only for new rows whose source data does not carry an external Id. 6 options / 2 examples

Usage

meta bulk-insert <Entity> [--from tsv|csv] [--file <path>|--stdin] [--key Field[,Field2...]] [--auto-id] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--from tsv|csv Input format. tsv|csv False
--file <path> Input file. <path> False
--stdin Read input from stdin. False
--key Field[,Field2...] Match key fields. Field[,Field2...] False
--auto-id Generate numeric Id only for input rows that omit Id and are being created. False
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta bulk-insert Cube --from tsv --file .\cube.tsv --key Id
meta bulk-insert Cube --from tsv --file .\cube-no-id.tsv --auto-id
meta delete Delete one instance by Id. 1 option / 1 example

Usage

meta delete <Entity> <Id> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta delete Cube 10
meta insert Insert one instance by explicit Id or auto-generated numeric Id. Use --auto-id only when creating a brand-new row with no external identity. 3 options / 2 examples

Usage

meta insert <Entity> [<Id>|--auto-id] --set Field=Value [--set Field=Value ...] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--auto-id Generate next numeric Id from existing instances for a new row when no external Id exists. False
--set Field=Value Set property/relationship values. Field=Value False
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta insert Cube 10 --set "CubeName=Ops Cube"
meta insert Cube --auto-id --set "CubeName=Ops Cube"
meta instance Diff/merge instances and apply instance updates. 1 option / 4 examples

Usage

meta instance <diff|merge|diff-aligned|merge-aligned|update|rename-id|relationship> ...

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta instance update Cube 1 --set RefreshMode=Manual
meta instance rename-id Cube 1 Cube-001
meta instance relationship set Measure 1 --to Cube 2
meta instance diff .\LeftWorkspace .\RightWorkspace
meta query Search instances using equals/contains filters. 4 options / 2 examples

Usage

meta query <Entity> [--equals <Field> <Value>]... [--contains <Field> <Value>]... [--top <n>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--equals <Field> <Value> Exact field value match (repeatable). <Field> <Value> False
--contains <Field> <Value> Contains match (repeatable). <Field> <Value> False
--top <n> Limit preview instances (default 200). <n> False
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta query Cube --contains CubeName Sales
meta query Cube --equals Id 1 --top 20
meta check Run model + instance integrity checks. 1 option / 1 example

Usage

meta check [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta check
meta graph Inspect relationship graph structure. 1 option / 2 examples

Usage

meta graph <stats|inbound> ...

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta graph stats --top 10 --cycles 10
meta graph inbound Cube --top 20
meta list List entities, properties, or relationships. 1 option / 3 examples

Usage

meta list <entities|properties|relationships> ...

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta list entities
meta list properties Cube
meta list relationships Measure
meta model Inspect and edit model entities, properties, and relationships. 1 option / 5 examples

Usage

meta model <subcommand> [arguments] [options]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Workspace root override. <path> False

Examples

meta model suggest
meta model rename-model OldModel NewModel
meta model add-entity SalesCube
meta model rename-entity OldName NewName
meta model add-property Cube Purpose --required true --default-value Unknown

meta model add-entity <Name> [--workspace <path>]

Examples

meta model rename-model Rename the model contract and instance root element. 1 option / 1 example

meta model rename-model <Old> <New> [--workspace <path>]

Examples

meta model rename-entity Atomically rename an entity, update relationship targets, and rename implied non-role relationship fields. 1 option / 1 example

Usage

meta model rename-entity <Old> <New> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model rename-entity Warehouse StorageLocation
meta model drop-entity Drop an entity (blocked if instances or inbound relationships exist). 1 option / 1 example

Usage

meta model drop-entity <Entity> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model drop-entity SourceSystem
meta model add-property Add an entity property. 3 options / 1 example

Usage

meta model add-property <Entity> <Property> [--required true|false] [--default-value <Value>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--required true|false Set required/nullable state. true|false False
--default-value <Value> Backfill existing rows (required for required properties when entity already has rows). <Value> False
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model add-property Cube Purpose --required true --default-value Unknown
meta model set-property-required Change whether a property is required; use --default-value to backfill existing rows when making a property required. 3 options / 2 examples

Usage

meta model set-property-required <Entity> <Property> --required true|false [--default-value <Value>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--required true|false Required. Set required/nullable state. true|false True
--default-value <Value> Backfill existing missing/blank rows when switching to required; only valid with --required true. <Value> False
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model set-property-required Cube Purpose --required true --default-value Unknown
meta model set-property-required Cube Purpose --required false
meta model rename-property Rename a property in one entity. 1 option / 1 example

Usage

meta model rename-property <Entity> <Old> <New> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model rename-property Cube Purpose Description
meta model drop-property Drop a property from an entity. 1 option / 1 example

Usage

meta model drop-property <Entity> <Property> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model drop-property Cube Description
meta model rename-relationship Rename a relationship usage by setting or clearing its role; instance relationship field names are rewritten atomically. 2 options / 2 examples

Usage

meta model rename-relationship <FromEntity> <ToEntity> [--role <Role>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--role <Role> Set a new relationship role (usage column becomes <Role>Id). Omit --role to clear the role and revert usage naming to <TargetEntity>Id. <Role> False
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model rename-relationship System SystemType --role PrimarySystemType
meta model rename-relationship System SystemType
meta model add-relationship Add a required relationship; use --default-id to backfill existing source instances. 3 options / 1 example

Usage

meta model add-relationship <FromEntity> <ToEntity> [--role <RoleName>] [--default-id <ToId>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--role <RoleName> Optional relationship role (column becomes <RoleName>Id). <RoleName> False
--default-id <ToId> Required when source entity already has rows; must exist in target entity. <ToId> True
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model add-relationship Measure Cube --default-id 1
meta model refactor Run atomic model+instance refactors. 1 option / 1 example

Usage

meta model refactor <subcommand> [arguments] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model refactor property-to-relationship --source Order.WarehouseId --target Warehouse --lookup Id
meta model refactor property-to-relationship Atomically convert a scalar source property to a required relationship using a target lookup key. 6 options / 2 examples

Usage

meta model refactor property-to-relationship --source <Entity.Property> --target <Entity> --lookup <Property> [--role <Role>] [--preserve-property] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--source <Entity.Property> Required source scalar property to rewrite. <Entity.Property> True
--target <Entity> Required target entity. <Entity> True
--lookup <Property> Required lookup key property on target entity. <Property> True
--role <Role> Optional relationship role (usage column becomes <Role>Id). <Role> False
--preserve-property Keep the source scalar property. This is only valid when the source property name does not collide with the implied relationship usage name. False
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model refactor property-to-relationship --source Order.WarehouseId --target Warehouse --lookup Id
meta model refactor property-to-relationship --source Order.ProductId --target Product --lookup Id --role ProductRef --preserve-property
meta model refactor relationship-to-property Atomically convert a required relationship back to a required scalar Id property. 5 options / 2 examples

Usage

meta model refactor relationship-to-property --source <Entity> --target <Entity> [--role <Role>] [--property <PropertyName>] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--source <Entity> Required source entity that owns the relationship. <Entity> True
--target <Entity> Required target entity referenced by the relationship. <Entity> True
--role <Role> Optional relationship role when multiple edges target the same entity. <Role> False
--property <PropertyName> Optional replacement scalar property name (default: <RoleOrTarget>Id). <PropertyName> False
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model refactor relationship-to-property --source Order --target Warehouse
meta model refactor relationship-to-property --source Order --target Product --role ProductRef --property ProductId
meta model drop-relationship Drop a declared relationship (blocked if in use). 1 option / 1 example

Usage

meta model drop-relationship <FromEntity> <ToEntity> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model drop-relationship Measure Cube
meta model suggest Read-only relationship inference from model + instance data. Strong suggestions require one exact eligible Id-based target; weak suggestions cover role-style suffix matches and other ambiguous multi-target cases. 4 options / 3 examples

Usage

meta model suggest [--show-keys] [--explain] [--print-commands] [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--show-keys Also print candidate business keys. False
--explain Include Evidence/Stats/Why detail blocks. False
--print-commands Print copy/paste refactor commands for eligible relationship suggestions. False
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta model suggest --workspace .\Workspace
meta model suggest --show-keys --explain --workspace .\Workspace
meta model suggest --print-commands --workspace .\Workspace
meta view View entity schema or one instance. 1 option / 2 examples

Usage

meta view <entity|instance> ...

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta view entity Cube
meta view instance Cube 1
meta deploy Deploy generated artifacts to external targets. 3 options / 1 example

Usage

meta deploy <sqlserver> ...

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--scripts <dir> Input directory containing generated SQL scripts. <dir> False
--connection-env <name> Environment variable name that contains the target SQL Server connection string. <name> False
--database <name> Optional database to create/use before deployment. <name> False

Examples

meta deploy sqlserver --scripts .\out\sql --connection-env META_SQL_DEV --database DemoDv
meta export Export workspace data to external formats. 1 option / 1 example

Usage

meta export <csv> ...

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta export csv Cube --out .\cube.csv
meta generate Generate artifacts from the workspace. 3 options / 4 examples

Usage

meta generate <sql|csharp|ssdt> --out <dir> [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--out <dir> Output directory. <dir> False
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False
--tooling C# only. Emit optional tooling helpers in <Model>.Tooling.cs. False

Examples

meta generate sql --out .\out\sql
meta generate csharp --out .\out\csharp
meta generate csharp --out .\out\csharp --tooling
meta generate ssdt --out .\out\ssdt
meta import Import from SQL into a new workspace, or import CSV into new/existing workspace. 2 options / 2 examples

Usage

meta import <sql|csv> ...

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--new-workspace <path> Required for sql; optional for csv (mutually exclusive with --workspace). <path> True
--workspace <path> Use existing workspace for csv import. <path> False

Examples

meta import sql --connection-env META_SQL_DEV dbo --new-workspace .\ImportedWorkspace
meta import csv .\landing.csv --entity Landing --new-workspace .\ImportedWorkspace
meta init Initialize a metadata workspace. 0 options / 2 examples

Usage

meta init [<path>]

Examples

meta init .
meta init .\Workspace
meta status Show workspace summary and model/instance sizes. 1 option / 2 examples

Usage

meta status [--workspace <path>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Override workspace root. <path> False

Examples

meta status
meta status --workspace .\Workspace
meta workspace Workspace-level operations. 0 options / 1 example

Usage

meta workspace <merge> ...

Examples

meta workspace merge .\LeftWorkspace .\RightWorkspace --new-workspace .\MergedWorkspace --model MergedModel
Meta / CLI

meta-docs

`meta-docs` turns CLI definitions, workspace models, selected instance facts, and authored narrative into refreshable reference documentation.

Commands

11 commands exposed by meta-docs.

CommandSummary
meta-docs author-page Add or update an authored documentation page in a MetaDocs workspace.
meta-docs import-cli Import a structured CLI definition into a MetaDocs workspace.
meta-docs import-command-prose Import command-oriented markdown prose into existing CLI documentation subjects.
meta-docs import-workspace-model Import generic model documentation from a Meta workspace.
meta-docs import-workspace-instances Import selected workspace instance documentation from a modeled opt-in policy.
meta-docs include-instance-entity Add or update an opt-in entity policy for workspace instance documentation.
meta-docs include-instance-property Add or update an opt-in property policy for workspace instance documentation.
meta-docs include-instance-relationship Add or update an opt-in relationship policy for workspace instance documentation.
meta-docs merge Merge multiple MetaDocs workspaces into a suite workspace.
meta-docs validate Validate a MetaDocs workspace lifecycle and reference integrity.
meta-docs render-site Render one merged metametabi docs page from a MetaDocs workspace.
meta-docs author-page Add or update an authored documentation page in a MetaDocs workspace. 13 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs author-page (--workspace <path> | --new-workspace <path>) --id <id> --title <text> --summary <text> --body <text> [--kind <name>] [--path <text>] [--parent <id>] [--ordinal <n>] [--slot <name>] [--source-id <id>] [--source-name <text>]

Guidance

This writes DocumentationSubject, DocumentationNarrative, and DocumentationViewNode rows.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Existing MetaDocs workspace to update in place. <path> False
--new-workspace <path> New MetaDocs workspace to create. <path> False
--id <id> Required. Stable documentation subject id. <id> True
--title <text> Required. Page title and subject display name. <text> True
--summary <text> Required. Short subject summary. <text> True
--body <text> Required. Authored narrative body. <text> True
--kind <name> Optional subject kind. Default: Guide. <name> False
--path <text> Optional display path. Default: title. <text> False
--parent <id> Optional parent subject id. <id> False
--ordinal <n> Optional page ordering number. Default: 100. <n> False
--slot <name> Optional narrative slot. Default: Summary. <name> False
--source-id <id> Optional authored documentation source id. <id> False
--source-name <text> Optional authored documentation source display name. <text> False
meta-docs import-cli Import a structured CLI definition into a MetaDocs workspace. 8 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs import-cli --assembly <path> --type <name> [--method <name>] (--workspace <path> | --new-workspace <path>) [--group <name>] [--ordinal <n>] [--source-id <id>]

Guidance

CLI facts are refreshed from the definition assembly.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--assembly <path> Required. Assembly that exposes a public static CliAppDefinition factory method. <path> True
--type <name> Required. Fully qualified type name containing the factory method. <name> True
--method <name> Optional. Public static factory method name. Default: CreateAppDefinition. <name> False
--workspace <path> Existing MetaDocs workspace to update in place. <path> False
--new-workspace <path> New MetaDocs workspace to create. <path> False
--group <name> Optional rendered group name, for example meta-bi. <name> False
--ordinal <n> Optional application ordering number. Default: 100. <n> False
--source-id <id> Optional stable documentation source id. <id> False
meta-docs import-command-prose Import command-oriented markdown prose into existing CLI documentation subjects. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs import-command-prose --workspace <path> --source-root <path> [--source-id <id>]

Guidance

Markdown headings are matched to CLI application and command names.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. Existing MetaDocs workspace that already contains imported CLI subjects. <path> True
--source-root <path> Required. Markdown file or directory to scan for command prose. <path> True
--source-id <id> Optional stable documentation source id for imported markdown prose. <id> False
meta-docs import-workspace-model Import generic model documentation from a Meta workspace. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs import-workspace-model --source-workspace <path> (--workspace <path> | --new-workspace <path>) [--source-id <id>] [--display-name <name>] [--ordinal <n>]

Guidance

This imports model structure only: workspace, model, entities, properties, and relationships.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--source-workspace <path> Required. Source Meta workspace whose model should be documented. <path> True
--workspace <path> Existing MetaDocs workspace to update in place. <path> False
--new-workspace <path> New MetaDocs workspace to create. <path> False
--source-id <id> Optional stable documentation source id. <id> False
--display-name <name> Optional source display name. <name> False
--ordinal <n> Optional root subject ordering number. Default: 100. <n> False
meta-docs import-workspace-instances Import selected workspace instance documentation from a modeled opt-in policy. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs import-workspace-instances --source-workspace <path> --workspace <path> [--source-id <id>] [--model-source-id <id>] [--display-name <name>] [--ordinal <n>]

Guidance

Default policy imports no instance data.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--source-workspace <path> Required. Source Meta workspace whose allowed instances should be documented. <path> True
--workspace <path> Required. Existing MetaDocs workspace to update in place. <path> True
--source-id <id> Optional stable documentation source id for imported instance docs. <id> False
--model-source-id <id> Optional source id used by import-workspace-model, for linking instance docs to model docs. <id> False
--display-name <name> Optional source display name. <name> False
--ordinal <n> Optional root subject ordering number. Default: 200. <n> False
meta-docs include-instance-entity Add or update an opt-in entity policy for workspace instance documentation. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs include-instance-entity --workspace <path> --entity <name> [--source-id <id>] [--display-name-property <name>] [--summary-property <name>] [--ordinal <n>]

Guidance

This writes modeled MetaDocs policy rows. It does not import instance values by itself.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. Existing MetaDocs workspace to update in place. <path> True
--entity <name> Required. Source workspace entity name to include. <name> True
--source-id <id> Optional model documentation source id this policy applies to. Omit for a global policy. <id> False
--display-name-property <name> Optional property used only as the imported instance display name. <name> False
--summary-property <name> Optional property used only as the imported instance summary. <name> False
--ordinal <n> Optional policy ordering number. Default: 100. <n> False
meta-docs include-instance-property Add or update an opt-in property policy for workspace instance documentation. 5 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs include-instance-property --workspace <path> --entity <name> --property <name> [--source-id <id>] [--ordinal <n>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. Existing MetaDocs workspace to update in place. <path> True
--entity <name> Required. Source workspace entity name. <name> True
--property <name> Required. Property name to import as a generated fact. <name> True
--source-id <id> Optional model documentation source id this policy applies to. Omit for a global policy. <id> False
--ordinal <n> Optional policy ordering number. Default: 100. <n> False
meta-docs include-instance-relationship Add or update an opt-in relationship policy for workspace instance documentation. 5 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs include-instance-relationship --workspace <path> --entity <name> --relationship <name> [--source-id <id>] [--ordinal <n>]

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. Existing MetaDocs workspace to update in place. <path> True
--entity <name> Required. Source workspace entity name. <name> True
--relationship <name> Required. Relationship selector, navigation name, or relationship column name. <name> True
--source-id <id> Optional model documentation source id this policy applies to. Omit for a global policy. <id> False
--ordinal <n> Optional policy ordering number. Default: 100. <n> False
meta-docs merge Merge multiple MetaDocs workspaces into a suite workspace. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs merge --include <workspace> [--include <workspace> ...] --new-workspace <path>

Guidance

Source ids are preserved. Duplicate default theme/view rows are canonicalized in the suite workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--include <workspace> Required. MetaDocs workspace to include. Repeat for many workspaces. <workspace> True
--new-workspace <path> Required. Suite workspace to create or replace. <path> True
meta-docs validate Validate a MetaDocs workspace lifecycle and reference integrity. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs validate --workspace <path> [--warnings-as-errors]

Guidance

Validation checks source/documentation references, view references, authored prose review status, duplicate display paths, and modeled theme assets/templates.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaDocs workspace to validate. <path> True
--warnings-as-errors Optional. Treat warnings as failing diagnostics. False
meta-docs render-site Render one merged metametabi docs page from a MetaDocs workspace. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-docs render-site --workspace <path> --out <dir>

Guidance

The renderer consumes DocumentationSubject, DocumentationFact, DocumentationNarrative, DocumentationView, and modeled theme assets.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaDocs workspace to render. <path> True
--out <dir> Required. Output directory. The merged page is written as docs.html. <dir> True
Meta / CLI

meta-weave

`meta-weave` creates MetaWeave workspaces for explicit model-to-model weaving, where structure is carried across modeled surfaces instead of hidden in ad hoc conversion code.

Commands

6 commands exposed by meta-weave.

CommandSummary
meta-weave init Create a new MetaWeave workspace.
meta-weave add-model Add a referenced model workspace.
meta-weave add-binding Add a property binding between two model references.
meta-weave suggest Suggest missing property bindings only when the source values resolve uniquely and completely in a target key.
meta-weave check Validate property bindings across referenced workspaces.
meta-weave materialize Materialize a new workspace from a valid weave.
meta-weave init Create a new MetaWeave workspace. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-weave init --new-workspace <path>

Guidance

Creates a new workspace with the MetaWeave model and validates it.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--new-workspace <path> Required. Empty target directory for the new MetaWeave workspace. <path> True
meta-weave add-model Add a referenced model workspace. 4 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-weave add-model --workspace <path> --alias <alias> --model <modelName> --workspace-path <path>

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaWeave workspace to update. <path> True
--alias <alias> Required. Local model alias used by bindings. <alias> True
--model <modelName> Required. Referenced model name. <modelName> True
--workspace-path <path> Required. Referenced model workspace path. <path> True
meta-weave add-binding Add a property binding between two model references. 8 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-weave add-binding --workspace <path> --name <bindingName> --source-model <alias> --source-entity <entity> --source-property <property> --target-model <alias> --target-entity <entity> --target-property <property>

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaWeave workspace to update. <path> True
--name <bindingName> Required. Binding identity. <bindingName> True
--source-model <alias> Required. Source model alias. <alias> True
--source-entity <entity> Required. Source entity name. <entity> True
--source-property <property> Required. Source property name. <property> True
--target-model <alias> Required. Target model alias. <alias> True
--target-entity <entity> Required. Target entity name. <entity> True
--target-property <property> Required. Target property name. <property> True
meta-weave suggest Suggest missing property bindings only when the source values resolve uniquely and completely in a target key. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-weave suggest --workspace <path>

Guidance

Strong suggestions require exact property-name alignment plus complete and unique resolution.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaWeave workspace to inspect. <path> True
meta-weave check Validate property bindings across referenced workspaces. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-weave check --workspace <path>

Guidance

Loads referenced workspaces and validates that every bound source property resolves exactly once in the target model.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaWeave workspace to validate. <path> True
meta-weave materialize Materialize a new workspace from a valid weave. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-weave materialize --workspace <path> --new-workspace <path> --model <name>

Guidance

Checks the weave, calls core workspace merge on the referenced workspaces, and materializes weave bindings as in-workspace relationships.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaWeave workspace to materialize from. <path> True
--new-workspace <path> Required. Empty target directory for the materialized workspace. <path> True
--model <name> Required. Materialized model name. <name> True

Meta-BI / CLI

Meta-BI CLI

BI modeling and conversion command-line tools.

Command-line tools

Select an item from the navigation to open its reference page.

CLISummary
meta-analytics `meta-analytics` owns common analytics concepts that can be realized through target-specific tabular or multidimensional models.
meta-convert `meta-convert` performs explicit cross-model conversions between supported Meta-BI workspaces, carrying structure from one modeled surface into another.
meta-data-quality `meta-data-quality` derives reviewable MetaDataQuality candidates from the typed semantic graph of MetaTransformScript workspaces and promotes them into executable quality assets.
meta-data-type `meta-data-type` bootstraps sanctioned MetaDataType workspaces that define the data-type vocabulary used across Meta-BI models and conversions.
meta-data-type-conversion `meta-data-type-conversion` authors and validates sanctioned conversion graphs between data types, including resolution from a source type through modeled conversion rules.
meta-data-warehouse `meta-data-warehouse` models logical dimensional warehouse concepts, separating warehouse intent from SQL Server realization and deployment mechanics.
meta-datavault-business `meta-datavault-business` creates sanctioned business Data Vault workspaces for modeling business-oriented vault structures.
meta-datavault-raw `meta-datavault-raw` creates sanctioned raw Data Vault workspaces for modeling source-oriented Data Vault structures.
meta-multi-dimensional `meta-multi-dimensional` creates sanctioned MetaMultiDimensional workspaces for modeling multidimensional analytics artifacts.
meta-orchestration `meta-orchestration` creates MetaOrchestration workspaces from modeled pipeline and binding metadata, turning execution structure into inspectable orchestration data.
meta-pipeline `meta-pipeline` creates sanctioned MetaPipeline workspaces that describe transform steps and pipeline structure as explicit metadata.
meta-schema `meta-schema` extracts live SQL Server schema structure into sanctioned MetaSchema workspaces, making database objects available as explicit metadata.
meta-sql `meta-sql` plans and applies manifest-driven SQL Server deployments from sanctioned MetaSql workspaces.
meta-tabular `meta-tabular` creates sanctioned MetaTabular workspaces for modeling tabular analytics artifacts.
meta-transform-binding `meta-transform-binding` binds MetaTransformScript workspaces to explicit source and target schema contracts, producing rowset, column, and validation evidence.
meta-transform-script `meta-transform-script` models a bounded SQL transform surface as semantically round-trippable workspace data, preserving transform intent through import, emit, and diff cycles.

Meta-BI / CLI

meta-analytics

`meta-analytics` owns common analytics concepts that can be realized through target-specific tabular or multidimensional models.

Commands

28 commands exposed by meta-analytics.

CommandSummary
meta-analytics --new-workspace Create an empty MetaAnalytics workspace.
meta-analytics add-aggregation-behavior Declare a base measure aggregate function.
meta-analytics add-attribute Add a typed table attribute or calculated attribute.
meta-analytics add-attribute-permission Add object-level security for an attribute.
meta-analytics add-attribute-relationship Declare an attribute relationship inside a table.
meta-analytics add-attribute-translation Translate attribute metadata.
meta-analytics add-culture Add a model culture.
meta-analytics add-data-source Add an analytics source declaration.
meta-analytics add-hierarchy Add a hierarchy.
meta-analytics add-hierarchy-level Add an ordered hierarchy level.
meta-analytics add-hierarchy-translation Translate hierarchy metadata.
meta-analytics add-measure Add a source-backed base measure.
meta-analytics add-measure-translation Translate measure metadata.
meta-analytics add-model Add an analytics model.
meta-analytics add-perspective Add a perspective.
meta-analytics add-perspective-attribute Expose an attribute in a perspective.
meta-analytics add-perspective-hierarchy Expose a hierarchy in a perspective.
meta-analytics add-perspective-measure Expose a measure in a perspective.
meta-analytics add-perspective-table Expose a table in a perspective.
meta-analytics add-perspective-translation Translate perspective metadata.
meta-analytics add-relationship Add a relationship between analytics tables.
meta-analytics add-role-filter Add row-level security over a table.
meta-analytics add-role-member Add a member to a security role.
meta-analytics add-security-role Add a security role.
meta-analytics add-sort-by-attribute Declare one attribute as the sort key for another.
meta-analytics add-table Add an analytics table.
meta-analytics add-table-permission Add object-level security for a table.
meta-analytics add-table-translation Translate table metadata.
meta-analytics --new-workspace Create an empty MetaAnalytics workspace. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-analytics --new-workspace <path>

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired

Meta-BI / CLI

meta-convert

`meta-convert` performs explicit cross-model conversions between supported Meta-BI workspaces, carrying structure from one modeled surface into another.

Commands

9 commands exposed by meta-convert.

CommandSummary
meta-convert schema-to-raw-datavault Convert MetaSchema workspace to MetaRawDataVault workspace.
meta-convert raw-datavault-to-sql Convert MetaRawDataVault workspace to MetaSql workspace.
meta-convert business-datavault-to-sql Convert MetaBusinessDataVault workspace to MetaSql workspace.
meta-convert data-quality-to-sql Convert promoted MetaDataQuality candidates to SQL DQ views.
meta-convert data-warehouse-to-sql Convert MetaDataWarehouse workspace to MetaSql workspace.
meta-convert transform-script-to-sql Convert MetaTransformScript SQL modules to MetaSql workspace.
meta-convert sql-to-transform-script Convert MetaSql SQL modules to MetaTransformScript workspace.
meta-convert analytics-to-tabular Convert MetaAnalytics workspace to MetaTabular workspace.
meta-convert analytics-to-multi-dimensional Convert MetaAnalytics workspace to MetaMultiDimensional workspace.
meta-convert schema-to-raw-datavault Convert MetaSchema workspace to MetaRawDataVault workspace. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-convert schema-to-raw-datavault --source-workspace <path> --new-workspace <path> [--ignore-field-name <name>]... [--ignore-field-suffix <suffix>]... [--include-views] [--verbose]

Guidance

Loads MetaSchema from --source-workspace and saves MetaRawDataVault at --new-workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--source-workspace <path> Required. MetaSchema workspace to convert. <path> True
--new-workspace <path> Required. Directory where the MetaRawDataVault workspace will be created. <path> True
--ignore-field-name <name> Optional source field name to ignore. May be repeated. <name> False
--ignore-field-suffix <suffix> Optional source field suffix to ignore. May be repeated. <suffix> False
--include-views Optional. Include source views. False
--verbose Optional. Print conversion summary. False
meta-convert raw-datavault-to-sql Convert MetaRawDataVault workspace to MetaSql workspace. 4 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-convert raw-datavault-to-sql [--workspace <path>] --implementation-workspace <path> --database-name <name> --out <path>

Guidance

Converts the current sanctioned MetaRawDataVault workspace to a current MetaSql workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Optional. Source workspace path. Default: current working directory. <path> False
--implementation-workspace <path> Required. Implementation policy workspace. <path> True
--database-name <name> Required. Target MetaSql database name. <name> True
--out <path> Required. Output MetaSql workspace path. <path> True
meta-convert business-datavault-to-sql Convert MetaBusinessDataVault workspace to MetaSql workspace. 4 options / 0 examples

--implementation-workspace <path> Required. Implementation policy workspace. <path> True

--out <path> Required. Output MetaSql workspace path. <path> True

meta-convert transform-script-to-sql Convert MetaTransformScript SQL modules to MetaSql workspace. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-convert transform-script-to-sql [--workspace <path>] --database-name <name> --out <path>

Guidance

Converts MetaTransformScript view, function, and stored procedure modules to a current MetaSql workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Optional. Source MetaTransformScript workspace path. Default: current working directory. <path> False
--database-name <name> Required. Target MetaSql database name. <name> True
--out <path> Required. Output MetaSql workspace path. <path> True
meta-convert sql-to-transform-script Convert MetaSql SQL modules to MetaTransformScript workspace. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-convert sql-to-transform-script [--workspace <path>] --out <path> [--include-views] [--include-functions] [--include-stored-procedures] [--allow-empty]

Guidance

Reads view, function, and stored procedure module definitions from a MetaSql workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Optional. Source MetaSql workspace path. Default: current working directory. <path> False
--out <path> Required. Output MetaTransformScript workspace path. <path> True
--include-views Convert view modules. If no include switch is provided, all module kinds are selected. False
--include-functions Convert function modules. If no include switch is provided, all module kinds are selected. False
--include-stored-procedures Convert stored procedure modules. If no include switch is provided, all module kinds are selected. False
--allow-empty Create an empty MetaTransformScript workspace when selected module kinds have no convertible modules. False
meta-convert analytics-to-tabular Convert MetaAnalytics workspace to MetaTabular workspace. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-convert analytics-to-tabular [--workspace <path>] --out <path>

Guidance

Converts common MetaAnalytics intent to a MetaTabular workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Optional. MetaAnalytics workspace to convert. Default: current working directory. <path> False
--out <path> Required. Output workspace or script folder path. <path> True
meta-convert analytics-to-multi-dimensional Convert MetaAnalytics workspace to MetaMultiDimensional workspace. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-convert analytics-to-multi-dimensional [--workspace <path>] --out <path>

--out <path> Required. Output workspace or script folder path. <path> True
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-data-quality

`meta-data-quality` derives reviewable MetaDataQuality candidates from the typed semantic graph of MetaTransformScript workspaces and promotes them into executable quality assets.

Commands

3 commands exposed by meta-data-quality.

CommandSummary
meta-data-quality from-transform-workspace Create generated DQ views from a full MetaTransformScript workspace.
meta-data-quality inspect Review the generated DQ pack and optional adjustments.
meta-data-quality promote Promote generated DQ candidates for SQL output.
meta-data-quality from-transform-workspace Create generated DQ views from a full MetaTransformScript workspace. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-data-quality from-transform-workspace --transform-workspace <path> --new-workspace <path> [--binding-workspace <path>]

Guidance

Scans all TransformScript instances in one workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--transform-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformScript workspace to analyze. <path> True
--new-workspace <path> Required. Directory where the generated MetaDataQuality workspace will be created. <path> True
--binding-workspace <path> Optional. MetaTransformBinding workspace used to scan only validated scripts. <path> False
meta-data-quality inspect Review the generated DQ pack and optional adjustments. 4 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-data-quality inspect --workspace <path> [--show-cases] [--top-cases <n>] [--show-candidate-ids]

Guidance

Default output guides the full-pack-first path.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaDataQuality workspace to inspect. <path> True
--show-cases Optional. Show candidate adjustment cases. False
--top-cases <n> Optional. Show up to n candidate cases. Implies --show-cases. Default: 20. <n> False
--show-candidate-ids Optional. Include candidate ids in adjustment output. Implies --show-cases. False
meta-data-quality promote Promote generated DQ candidates for SQL output. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-data-quality promote --workspace <path> (--all | --candidate-id <id> [--candidate-id <id> ...])

Guidance

Promotes generated DQ candidates for data-quality-to-sql output.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaDataQuality workspace to update. <path> True
--all Promote every generated data-quality candidate. False
--candidate-id <id> Promote one generated candidate. May be provided more than once. <id> False
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-data-type

`meta-data-type` bootstraps sanctioned MetaDataType workspaces that define the data-type vocabulary used across Meta-BI models and conversions.

Commands

1 command exposed by meta-data-type.

CommandSummary
meta-data-type --new-workspace Create a new MetaDataType workspace.
meta-data-type --new-workspace Create a new MetaDataType workspace. 1 option / 1 example

Usage

meta-data-type --new-workspace <path>

Guidance

bootstrap sanctioned `MetaDataType` workspaces

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--new-workspace <path> Required. Directory where the sanctioned workspace will be created. <path> True

Examples

meta-data-type --new-workspace .\MetaDataType.Workspace
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-data-type-conversion

`meta-data-type-conversion` authors and validates sanctioned conversion graphs between data types, including resolution from a source type through modeled conversion rules.

Commands

3 commands exposed by meta-data-type-conversion.

CommandSummary
meta-data-type-conversion --new-workspace Create a new MetaDataTypeConversion workspace.
meta-data-type-conversion check Validate sanctioned type mappings.
meta-data-type-conversion resolve Resolve one source data type id through the sanctioned mappings.
meta-data-type-conversion --new-workspace Create a new MetaDataTypeConversion workspace. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-data-type-conversion --new-workspace <path>

Guidance

Creates a new workspace with the MetaDataTypeConversion model and validates it.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--new-workspace <path> Required. Directory where the sanctioned workspace will be created. <path> True
meta-data-type-conversion check Validate sanctioned type mappings. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-data-type-conversion check --workspace <path>

Guidance

Validates that each source data type maps deterministically per target data type system and that every mapping references a real ConversionImplementation.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaDataTypeConversion workspace to validate. <path> True
meta-data-type-conversion resolve Resolve one source data type id through the sanctioned mappings. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-data-type-conversion resolve --workspace <path> --source-data-type <id> [--target-data-type-system <name>]

Guidance

Resolves one source data type id to its target data type id and conversion implementation.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaDataTypeConversion workspace to query. <path> True
--source-data-type <id> Required. Source data type id to resolve. <id> True
--target-data-type-system <name> Optional target system when the source type has mappings to several target systems. <name> False
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-data-warehouse

`meta-data-warehouse` models logical dimensional warehouse concepts, separating warehouse intent from SQL Server realization and deployment mechanics.

Commands

31 commands exposed by meta-data-warehouse.

CommandSummary
meta-data-warehouse --new-workspace Create an empty MetaDataWarehouse workspace.
meta-data-warehouse add-accumulating-snapshot-fact Mark a fact as an accumulating snapshot.
meta-data-warehouse add-accumulating-snapshot-milestone Add a lifecycle milestone to an accumulating snapshot.
meta-data-warehouse add-aggregate-fact Declare an aggregate fact derived from a source fact.
meta-data-warehouse add-bridge Add a dimensional bridge table.
meta-data-warehouse add-bridge-participant Add a dimension participant to a bridge.
meta-data-warehouse add-bridge-weight Add a bridge weighting measure with a Meta data type.
meta-data-warehouse add-conformed-dimension Mark a dimension as conformed.

meta-data-warehouse add-type2-dimension-attribute Declare a Type 2 attribute in an SCD dimension.

Meta-BI / CLI

meta-datavault-business

`meta-datavault-business` creates sanctioned business Data Vault workspaces for modeling business-oriented vault structures.

Commands

26 commands exposed by meta-datavault-business.

CommandSummary
meta-datavault-business --new-workspace Create an empty MetaBusinessDataVault workspace.
meta-datavault-business add-bridge Add a business bridge.
meta-datavault-business add-bridge-hub Add a business hub to an ordered bridge path.
meta-datavault-business add-bridge-link Add a business link to an ordered bridge path.
meta-datavault-business add-hierarchical-link Add a hierarchical link.
meta-datavault-business add-hierarchical-link-satellite Add a hierarchical link satellite.
meta-datavault-business add-hierarchical-link-satellite-attribute Add a hierarchical link satellite attribute.
meta-datavault-business add-hub Add a business hub.
meta-datavault-business add-hub-key-part Add a business hub key part.
meta-datavault-business add-hub-satellite Add a business hub satellite.
meta-datavault-business add-hub-satellite-attribute Add a business hub satellite attribute.
meta-datavault-business add-link Add a standard business link.
meta-datavault-business add-link-hub Add a participating hub to a standard business link.
meta-datavault-business add-link-satellite Add a business link satellite.
meta-datavault-business add-link-satellite-attribute Add a business link satellite attribute.
meta-datavault-business add-point-in-time Add a business point-in-time table.
meta-datavault-business add-point-in-time-hub-satellite Add a hub-satellite reference to a point-in-time table.
meta-datavault-business add-point-in-time-link-satellite Add a link-satellite reference to a point-in-time table.
meta-datavault-business add-point-in-time-stamp Add a business point-in-time stamp column.
meta-datavault-business add-reference Add a business reference.
meta-datavault-business add-reference-key-part Add a business reference key part.
meta-datavault-business add-reference-satellite Add a business reference satellite.
meta-datavault-business add-reference-satellite-attribute Add a business reference satellite attribute.
meta-datavault-business add-same-as-link Add a same-as link.
meta-datavault-business add-same-as-link-satellite Add a same-as link satellite.
meta-datavault-business add-same-as-link-satellite-attribute Add a same-as link satellite attribute.
meta-datavault-business --new-workspace Create an empty MetaBusinessDataVault workspace. 1 option / 0 examples

meta-datavault-business --new-workspace <path>

Meta-BI / CLI

meta-datavault-raw

`meta-datavault-raw` creates sanctioned raw Data Vault workspaces for modeling source-oriented Data Vault structures.

Commands

16 commands exposed by meta-datavault-raw.

CommandSummary
meta-datavault-raw --new-workspace Create an empty MetaRawDataVault workspace.
meta-datavault-raw add-hub Add a raw hub.
meta-datavault-raw add-hub-key-part Add a raw hub key part.
meta-datavault-raw add-hub-satellite Add a raw hub satellite.
meta-datavault-raw add-hub-satellite-attribute Add a raw hub satellite attribute.
meta-datavault-raw add-link Add a raw link.
meta-datavault-raw add-link-hub Add a participating hub to a raw link.
meta-datavault-raw add-link-satellite Add a raw link satellite.
meta-datavault-raw add-link-satellite-attribute Add a raw link satellite attribute.
meta-datavault-raw add-source-field Add a source field.
meta-datavault-raw add-source-field-data-type-detail Add a source field datatype detail.
meta-datavault-raw add-source-schema Add a source schema.
meta-datavault-raw add-source-system Add a source system.
meta-datavault-raw add-source-table Add a source table.
meta-datavault-raw add-source-table-relationship Add a source table relationship.
meta-datavault-raw add-source-table-relationship-field Add a source table relationship field.
meta-datavault-raw --new-workspace Create an empty MetaRawDataVault workspace. 1 option / 0 examples

meta-datavault-raw --new-workspace <path>

OptionDescriptionValueRequired

Adds one SourceTableRelationshipField row to a MetaRawDataVault workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Optional. Workspace path. Default: current working directory. <path> False
--id <id> Required. SourceTableRelationshipField row id. <id> True
--ordinal <value> Optional. Ordinal. <value> False
--relationship <id> Required. SourceTableRelationship id for SourceTableRelationshipId. <id> True
--source-field <id> Required. SourceField id for SourceFieldId. <id> True
--target-field <id> Required. SourceField id for TargetFieldId. <id> True
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-multi-dimensional

`meta-multi-dimensional` creates sanctioned MetaMultiDimensional workspaces for modeling multidimensional analytics artifacts.

Commands

42 commands exposed by meta-multi-dimensional.

CommandSummary
meta-multi-dimensional --new-workspace Create an empty MetaMultiDimensional workspace.
meta-multi-dimensional deploy Create modeled objects on an Analysis Services multidimensional instance.
meta-multi-dimensional restore Promote a processed multidimensional database through backup and restore.
meta-multi-dimensional drop Drop a multidimensional database from an Analysis Services multidimensional instance.
meta-multi-dimensional add-action-translation Add a ActionTranslation row.
meta-multi-dimensional add-attribute-relationship Add a AttributeRelationship row.
meta-multi-dimensional add-attribute-translation Add a AttributeTranslation row.

meta-multi-dimensional add-cube Add a Cube row.

meta-multi-dimensional --new-workspace Create an empty MetaMultiDimensional workspace. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-multi-dimensional --new-workspace <path>

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--new-workspace <path> Required. Directory where the empty MetaMultiDimensional workspace will be created. <path> True
meta-multi-dimensional deploy Create modeled objects on an Analysis Services multidimensional instance. 5 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-multi-dimensional deploy [--workspace <path>] --server <server> [--database-name <name>] [--drop-existing] [--no-process]

Guidance

Creates multidimensional database objects on an Analysis Services multidimensional instance.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> MetaMultiDimensional workspace to deploy. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
--server <server> Required. Analysis Services multidimensional server. <server> True
--database-name <name> Optional target database name. Defaults to the modeled database name. <name> False
--drop-existing Drop an existing target database before create/deploy. False
--no-process Deploy metadata only and skip full processing. False
meta-multi-dimensional restore Promote a processed multidimensional database through backup and restore. 7 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-multi-dimensional restore --source-server <server> --source-database-name <name> --target-server <server> --target-database-name <name> --backup-file <path> [--drop-existing] [--overwrite-backup-file]

Guidance

Backs up a processed multidimensional database and restores it as the target database.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--source-server <server> Required. Source Analysis Services server containing the processed database. <server> True
--source-database-name <name> Required. Source processed database name. <name> True
--target-server <server> Required. Target Analysis Services server. <server> True
--target-database-name <name> Required. Target database name to restore. <name> True
--backup-file <path> Required. Backup file path accessible to the Analysis Services service accounts. <path> True
--drop-existing Drop an existing target database before restore. False
--overwrite-backup-file Overwrite an existing backup file. False
meta-multi-dimensional drop Drop a multidimensional database from an Analysis Services multidimensional instance. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-multi-dimensional drop --server <server> --database-name <name>

Guidance

Drops a multidimensional database from an Analysis Services multidimensional instance.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--server <server> Required. Analysis Services multidimensional server. <server> True
--database-name <name> Required. Database name to drop. <name> True
