Meta-BI / CLI

meta-orchestration

`meta-orchestration` creates MetaOrchestration workspaces from modeled pipeline and binding metadata, turning execution structure into inspectable orchestration data.

Commands

10 commands exposed by meta-orchestration.

CommandSummary
meta-orchestration inspect Inspect a MetaOrchestration workspace.
meta-orchestration list-issues List analyzer issues recorded in an orchestration workspace.
meta-orchestration explain-issue Explain one analyzer issue and its participating pipelines.
meta-orchestration add-dependency Record an explicit success or failure dependency between tasks.
meta-orchestration add-order Record an explicit success dependency/order resolution in an orchestration workspace.
meta-orchestration allow-concurrent-append Allow concurrent execution for multiple Append effects on one object.
meta-orchestration set-lock-policy Record scoped lock compatibility for one object/effect interaction.
meta-orchestration refresh-run-plan Refresh lock-aware run-plan rows in an orchestration workspace.
meta-orchestration inspect-run-plan Inspect the planned task dependency graph.
meta-orchestration execute Execute the current run plan by coordinating meta-pipeline worker processes.
meta-orchestration inspect Inspect a MetaOrchestration workspace. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-orchestration inspect --workspace <path>

Guidance

Shows DAG, determinism, synchronization, dependency, effect, retry-policy, and issue summaries.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaOrchestration workspace to inspect. <path> True
meta-orchestration list-issues List analyzer issues recorded in an orchestration workspace. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-orchestration list-issues --workspace <path>

Guidance

Lists dependency, determinism, synchronization, and policy issues without changing analysis evidence.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaOrchestration workspace to inspect. <path> True
meta-orchestration explain-issue Explain one analyzer issue and its participating pipelines. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-orchestration explain-issue --workspace <path> --issue <id-or-unique-code>

Guidance

Shows issue domain, severity, blocking flags, object, message, and participating pipelines.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaOrchestration workspace to inspect. <path> True
--issue <id-or-unique-code> Required. Issue id or unique issue code. <id-or-unique-code> True
meta-orchestration add-dependency Record an explicit success or failure dependency between tasks. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-orchestration add-dependency --workspace <path> --from-task <task> --to-task <task> --condition success|failure [--object <sql-identifier>] [--reason <text>]

Guidance

Adds an explicit conditional DAG edge between planned tasks.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaOrchestration workspace to update. <path> True
--from-task <task> Required. Predecessor task selector. <task> True
--to-task <task> Required. Successor task selector. <task> True
--condition success|failure Required. Whether the successor follows predecessor success or failure. success|failure True
--object <sql-identifier> Optional object selector for object-scoped dependency resolution. <sql-identifier> False
--reason <text> Optional reason recorded with the policy row. <text> False
meta-orchestration add-order Record an explicit success dependency/order resolution in an orchestration workspace. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-orchestration add-order --workspace <path> --from-task <task> --to-task <task> [--condition success|failure] [--object <sql-identifier>] [--reason <text>]

Guidance

Adds an explicit task dependency resolution for a determinism issue.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaOrchestration workspace to update. <path> True
--from-task <task> Required. Predecessor task selector. <task> True
--to-task <task> Required. Successor task selector. <task> True
--condition success|failure Optional dependency condition. Default: success. success|failure False
--object <sql-identifier> Optional object selector for object-scoped dependency resolution. <sql-identifier> False
--reason <text> Optional reason recorded with the policy row. <text> False
meta-orchestration allow-concurrent-append Allow concurrent execution for multiple Append effects on one object. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-orchestration allow-concurrent-append --workspace <path> --object <sql-identifier> [--reason <text>]

Guidance

Adds a scoped Append/Append lock compatibility policy for concurrent append writers.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaOrchestration workspace to update. <path> True
--object <sql-identifier> Required. Data object whose append writers can overlap. <sql-identifier> True
--reason <text> Optional reason recorded with the policy row. <text> False
meta-orchestration set-lock-policy Record scoped lock compatibility for one object/effect interaction. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-orchestration set-lock-policy --workspace <path> --object <sql-identifier> --left-effect <effect> --right-effect <effect> --behavior <serialize|allow> [--reason <text>]

Guidance

Adds or updates scoped lock compatibility for an object/effect interaction.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaOrchestration workspace to update. <path> True
--object <sql-identifier> Required. Data object whose effect interaction is being resolved. <sql-identifier> True
--left-effect <effect> Required. Left write effect, such as Append, Replace, Mutation, KeyedUpsert, or ConditionalKeyedUpsert. <effect> True
--right-effect <effect> Required. Right write effect, such as Append, Replace, Mutation, KeyedUpsert, or ConditionalKeyedUpsert. <effect> True
--behavior <serialize|allow> Required. Lock behavior for the object/effect pair. <serialize|allow> True
--reason <text> Optional reason recorded with the policy row. <text> False
meta-orchestration refresh-run-plan Refresh lock-aware run-plan rows in an orchestration workspace. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-orchestration refresh-run-plan --workspace <path>

Guidance

Writes the run plan, default run-plan retry policy assignment, planned tasks, and task locks into the existing orchestration workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaOrchestration workspace to update. <path> True
meta-orchestration inspect-run-plan Inspect the planned task dependency graph. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-orchestration inspect-run-plan --workspace <path>

Guidance

Shows the task dependency graph as an adjacency list.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaOrchestration workspace to inspect. <path> True
meta-orchestration execute Execute the current run plan by coordinating meta-pipeline worker processes. 11 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-orchestration execute --workspace <path> --pipeline-workspace <path> --transform-workspace <path> --binding-workspace <path> [--data-type-conversion-workspace <path>] [--pipeline-db-connection-env <name>] [--max-degree-of-parallelism <n>] [--run-artifacts-root <path>] [--worker-event-timeout-seconds <n>] [--worker-activation-timeout-seconds <n>] [--worker-control-pipe-connect-timeout-seconds <n>]

Guidance

Refreshes run-plan rows from current workspace state, then executes the run plan.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaOrchestration workspace containing the analysis and run-plan rows. <path> True
--pipeline-workspace <path> Required. MetaPipeline workspace used by child pipeline workers. <path> True
--transform-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformScript workspace used by child pipeline workers. <path> True
--binding-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformBinding workspace used by child pipeline workers. <path> True
--data-type-conversion-workspace <path> Optional conversion policy workspace passed to child workers. <path> False
--pipeline-db-connection-env <name> Optional operational DB connection env passed to child workers. <name> False
--max-degree-of-parallelism <n> Maximum concurrently granted pipeline tasks. Default: 1. <n> False
--run-artifacts-root <path> Optional operational root for run journals, worker logs, and workspace execution leases. <path> False
--worker-event-timeout-seconds <n> Optional timeout for silent worker protocol periods. 0 or omitted means no timeout. <n> False
--worker-activation-timeout-seconds <n> Optional startup/activation timeout. Omitted follows --worker-event-timeout-seconds; 0 disables activation timeout. <n> False
--worker-control-pipe-connect-timeout-seconds <n> Optional timeout while waiting for child workers to connect to the named pipe. 0 or omitted means no timeout. <n> False
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-pipeline

`meta-pipeline` creates sanctioned MetaPipeline workspaces that describe transform steps and pipeline structure as explicit metadata.

Commands

9 commands exposed by meta-pipeline.

CommandSummary
meta-pipeline execute Execute a modeled pipeline's serial task chain.
meta-pipeline execute-worker Execute a modeled pipeline under an orchestration worker protocol.
meta-pipeline execute-step Execute one modeled transform-backed pipeline step.
meta-pipeline execute-sqlserver Execute the direct SQL Server runtime slice.
meta-pipeline create-pipeline-db Create or update the MetaPipeline operational DB.
meta-pipeline prune-pipeline-db Prune old MetaPipeline operational diagnostic logs.
meta-pipeline add-pipeline Add one Pipeline instance to a MetaPipeline workspace.
meta-pipeline add-step Add one transform-backed step to a pipeline.
meta-pipeline inspect Show a compact MetaPipeline workspace summary.
meta-pipeline execute Execute a modeled pipeline's serial task chain. 6 options / 1 example

Usage

meta-pipeline execute --workspace <path> --pipeline <name> --transform-workspace <path> --binding-workspace <path> [--data-type-conversion-workspace <path>] [--pipeline-db-connection-env <name>]

Guidance

Executes the serial PipelineTask chain declared in a MetaPipeline workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaPipeline workspace that contains the modeled serial task chain. <path> True
--pipeline <name> Required. Pipeline name to execute. <name> True
--transform-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformScript workspace used by transform tasks. <path> True
--binding-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformBinding workspace used by transform tasks. <path> True
--data-type-conversion-workspace <path> Optional conversion policy workspace; omitted uses the built-in defaults. <path> False
--pipeline-db-connection-env <name> Optional shell-visible environment variable for an initialized MetaPipeline operational DB. <name> False

Examples

meta-pipeline execute --workspace .\PipelineWS --pipeline CustomerLoad --transform-workspace .\TransformWS --binding-workspace .\BindingWS
meta-pipeline execute-worker Execute a modeled pipeline under an orchestration worker protocol. 8 options / 1 example

Usage

meta-pipeline execute-worker --workspace <path> --pipeline <name> --transform-workspace <path> --binding-workspace <path> --control-pipe <name> [--control-pipe-connect-timeout-seconds <n>] [--data-type-conversion-workspace <path>] [--pipeline-db-connection-env <name>]

Guidance

This command is an orchestration worker boundary, not an interactive user surface.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaPipeline workspace that contains the modeled serial task chain. <path> True
--pipeline <name> Required. Pipeline name to execute as a worker. <name> True
--transform-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformScript workspace used by transform tasks. <path> True
--binding-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformBinding workspace used by transform tasks. <path> True
--control-pipe <name> Required. Named pipe used for orchestration worker control messages. <name> True
--control-pipe-connect-timeout-seconds <n> Optional timeout while connecting to the orchestration control pipe. 0 or omitted means no timeout. <n> False
--data-type-conversion-workspace <path> Optional conversion policy workspace; omitted uses the built-in defaults. <path> False
--pipeline-db-connection-env <name> Optional shell-visible environment variable for an initialized MetaPipeline operational DB. <name> False

Examples

meta-pipeline execute-worker --workspace .\PipelineWS --pipeline CustomerLoad --transform-workspace .\TransformWS --binding-workspace .\BindingWS --control-pipe meta-worker-123
meta-pipeline execute-step Execute one modeled transform-backed pipeline step. 7 options / 1 example

Usage

meta-pipeline execute-step --workspace <path> --pipeline <name> --step-name <name-or-id> --transform-workspace <path> --binding-workspace <path> [--data-type-conversion-workspace <path>] [--pipeline-db-connection-env <name>]

Guidance

Executes exactly one transform-backed PipelineTask declared in a MetaPipeline workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaPipeline workspace that contains the modeled step. <path> True
--pipeline <name> Required. Pipeline name containing the step. <name> True
--step-name <name-or-id> Required. Pipeline task name or id to execute. <name-or-id> True
--transform-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformScript workspace used by the step. <path> True
--binding-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformBinding workspace used by the step. <path> True
--data-type-conversion-workspace <path> Optional conversion policy workspace; omitted uses the built-in defaults. <path> False
--pipeline-db-connection-env <name> Optional shell-visible environment variable for an initialized MetaPipeline operational DB. <name> False

Examples

meta-pipeline execute-step --workspace .\PipelineWS --pipeline CustomerLoad --step-name load-customers --transform-workspace .\TransformWS --binding-workspace .\BindingWS
meta-pipeline execute-sqlserver Execute the direct SQL Server runtime slice. 12 options / 1 example

Usage

meta-pipeline execute-sqlserver --transform-workspace <path> --binding-workspace <path> --script <name-or-id> [--binding <id>] --execution-connection-env <name> [--target-connection-env <name>] [--target <sql-identifier>] [--batch-size <n>] [--timeout-seconds <n>] [--target-data-type-system <name>] [--data-type-conversion-workspace <path>] [--pipeline-db-connection-env <name>]

Guidance

Executes one transform script against SQL Server.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--transform-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformScript workspace containing the script. <path> True
--binding-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformBinding workspace containing script binding rows. <path> True
--script <name-or-id> Required. TransformScript.Name or TransformScript.Id to execute. <name-or-id> True
--binding <id> Optional binding id when the selected script has multiple bindings. <id> False
--execution-connection-env <name> Required. Shell-visible environment variable for the execution SQL Server connection. <name> True
--target-connection-env <name> Required for SELECT-kind scripts. Shell-visible environment variable for the target SQL Server connection. <name> True
--target <sql-identifier> Target table identifier when a SELECT binding has multiple targets. <sql-identifier> False
--batch-size <n> Bounded in-memory row buffer size. Default: 1000. <n> False
--timeout-seconds <n> SQL command and bulk-copy timeout seconds. 0 or omitted means no command timeout. <n> False
--target-data-type-system <name> Runtime target type family for InsertRows. Default: SqlServer. <name> False
--data-type-conversion-workspace <path> Optional conversion policy workspace; omitted uses the built-in defaults. <path> False
--pipeline-db-connection-env <name> Optional shell-visible environment variable for an initialized MetaPipeline operational DB. <name> False

Examples

meta-pipeline execute-sqlserver --transform-workspace .\TransformWS --binding-workspace .\BindingWS --script dbo.v_customer_load --execution-connection-env EXECUTION_DB --target-connection-env TARGET_DB
meta-pipeline create-pipeline-db Create or update the MetaPipeline operational DB. 2 options / 1 example

Usage

meta-pipeline create-pipeline-db --pipeline-db-connection-env <name> [--pipeline-db-name <name>]

Guidance

Creates the SQL Server MetaPipeline operational database if needed and creates or updates its operational schema.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--pipeline-db-connection-env <name> Required. Shell-visible environment variable with a SQL Server connection string that can create the database. <name> True
--pipeline-db-name <name> Operational database name. Default: MetaPipeline. <name> False

Examples

meta-pipeline create-pipeline-db --pipeline-db-connection-env META_PIPELINE_SQLSERVER --pipeline-db-name MetaPipeline
meta-pipeline prune-pipeline-db Prune old MetaPipeline operational diagnostic logs. 3 options / 1 example

Usage

meta-pipeline prune-pipeline-db --pipeline-db-connection-env <name> --retention-days <days> [--dry-run]

Guidance

Deletes only RunDiagnosticsLog rows for completed runs older than the retention window.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--pipeline-db-connection-env <name> Required. Shell-visible environment variable for the initialized MetaPipeline operational DB. <name> True
--retention-days <days> Required. Delete eligible diagnostic rows older than this retention window. <days> True
--dry-run Report eligible rows without deleting them. False

Examples

meta-pipeline prune-pipeline-db --pipeline-db-connection-env META_PIPELINE_DB --retention-days 30 --dry-run
meta-pipeline add-pipeline Add one Pipeline instance to a MetaPipeline workspace. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-pipeline add-pipeline --workspace <path> --name <name> [--description <text>]

Guidance

Adds one Pipeline instance to an existing MetaPipeline workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. Existing MetaPipeline workspace to update. <path> True
--name <name> Required. Pipeline name. <name> True
--description <text> Optional pipeline description. <text> False
meta-pipeline add-step Add one transform-backed step to a pipeline. 14 options / 1 example

Usage

meta-pipeline add-step --workspace <path> --pipeline <name> --script <name-or-id> --transform-workspace <path> --binding-workspace <path> --execution-connection-env <name> [--step-name <name>] [--binding <id>] [--target-connection-env <name>] [--target <sql-identifier>] [--target-write <insert-rows>] [--batch-size <n>] [--timeout-seconds <n>] [--target-data-type-system <name>]

Guidance

Appends transform-backed task instances to the pipeline's serial task chain.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. Existing MetaPipeline workspace to update. <path> True
--pipeline <name> Required. Pipeline that receives the new serial step. <name> True
--script <name-or-id> Required. TransformScript.Name or TransformScript.Id to model. <name-or-id> True
--transform-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformScript workspace used for script selection. <path> True
--binding-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformBinding workspace used for binding selection. <path> True
--execution-connection-env <name> Required. Shell-visible environment variable named by the modeled execution connection. <name> True
--step-name <name> Optional step name; omitted derives a deterministic name from the script name. <name> False
--binding <id> Optional binding id when the selected script has multiple bindings. <id> False
--target-connection-env <name> Required for SELECT-kind scripts. Shell-visible environment variable named by the modeled target connection. <name> True
--target <sql-identifier> Target table identifier when a SELECT binding has multiple targets. <sql-identifier> False
--target-write <insert-rows> SELECT-kind target write model. The only supported value is insert-rows. <insert-rows> False
--batch-size <n> Bounded in-memory row buffer size for InsertRows. Default: 1000. <n> False
--timeout-seconds <n> SQL command and bulk-copy timeout seconds for the transform execution. 0 or omitted means no timeout. <n> False
--target-data-type-system <name> InsertRows target type family. Default: SqlServer. <name> False

Examples

meta-pipeline add-step --workspace .\PipelineWS --pipeline CustomerLoad --step-name load-customers --script dbo.v_customer_load --transform-workspace .\TransformWS --binding-workspace .\BindingWS --execution-connection-env EXECUTION_DB --target-connection-env TARGET_DB --target dbo.TargetCustomer --target-write insert-rows --batch-size 1000
meta-pipeline inspect Show a compact MetaPipeline workspace summary. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-pipeline inspect --workspace <path>

Guidance

Loads a MetaPipeline workspace and prints pipeline/task instance counts.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> Required. MetaPipeline workspace to inspect. <path> True
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-schema

`meta-schema` extracts live SQL Server schema structure into sanctioned MetaSchema workspaces, making database objects available as explicit metadata.

Commands

1 command exposed by meta-schema.

CommandSummary
meta-schema extract Materialize sanctioned MetaSchema workspaces from external sources.
meta-schema extract Materialize sanctioned MetaSchema workspaces from external sources. 0 options / 1 example

Usage

meta-schema extract <extractor> [options]

Guidance

extract a sanctioned `MetaSchema` workspace from a live SQL Server schema

Examples

meta-schema extract sqlserver --new-workspace .\MetaSchema.Workspace --connection-env META_SQL_DEV --system MySystem --schema dbo --all-tables
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-sql

`meta-sql` plans and applies manifest-driven SQL Server deployments from sanctioned MetaSql workspaces.

Commands

4 commands exposed by meta-sql.

CommandSummary
meta-sql extract Materialize sanctioned MetaSql workspaces from external sources.
meta-sql deploy-plan Create a deploy manifest (add/alter/block/replace; destructive actions require exact object-scoped approvals).
meta-sql deploy Apply a deploy manifest after source/live fingerprint validation.
meta-sql execute Execute a SQL Server file or query for demo/bootstrap/verification scripts.
meta-sql extract Materialize sanctioned MetaSql workspaces from external sources. 0 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-sql extract <extractor> [options]

Guidance

Available extractor: sqlserver.

meta-sql deploy-plan Create a deploy manifest (add/alter/block/replace; destructive actions require exact object-scoped approvals). 7 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-sql deploy-plan --source-workspace <path> --connection-env <name> --out <path> [--approve-drop-table <schema.table>] [--approve-drop-column <schema.table.column>] [--approve-truncate-column <schema.table.column>] [--approval-file <path>]

Guidance

Loads the source MetaSql workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--source-workspace <path> Required. Source MetaSql workspace to compare with the live database. <path> True
--connection-env <name> Required. Environment variable containing the SQL Server connection string. <name> True
--out <path> Required. Directory where the deploy manifest workspace will be written. <path> True
--approve-drop-table <schema.table> Approve one exact destructive table drop. <schema.table> False
--approve-drop-column <schema.table.column> Approve one exact destructive column drop. <schema.table.column> False
--approve-truncate-column <schema.table.column> Approve one exact destructive column truncation. <schema.table.column> False
--approval-file <path> Optional JSON file containing destructive approvals. <path> False
meta-sql deploy Apply a deploy manifest after source/live fingerprint validation. 3 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-sql deploy --manifest-workspace <path> --source-workspace <path> --connection-env <name>

Guidance

Loads the deploy manifest and source MetaSql workspace.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--manifest-workspace <path> Required. Deploy manifest workspace created by deploy-plan. <path> True
--source-workspace <path> Required. Source MetaSql workspace used to create the manifest. <path> True
--connection-env <name> Required. Environment variable containing the SQL Server connection string. <name> True
meta-sql execute Execute a SQL Server file or query for demo/bootstrap/verification scripts. 6 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-sql execute --connection-env <name> (--file <path> | --query <sql>) [--var <name=value>] [--timeout-seconds <seconds>] [--quiet]

Guidance

Executes SQL Server SQL batches for demo/bootstrap/verification scripts.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--connection-env <name> Required. Environment variable containing the SQL Server connection string. <name> True
--file <path> Execute SQL from a file. Mutually exclusive with --query. <path> False
--query <sql> Execute inline SQL. Mutually exclusive with --file. <sql> False
--var <name=value> Replace one SQLCMD-style $(NAME) token before execution. Can be repeated. <name=value> False
--timeout-seconds <seconds> Command timeout for each SQL batch. 0 or omitted means no timeout. <seconds> False
--quiet Suppress result-set and success output. False
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-tabular

`meta-tabular` creates sanctioned MetaTabular workspaces for modeling tabular analytics artifacts.

Commands

36 commands exposed by meta-tabular.

CommandSummary
meta-tabular --new-workspace Create an empty MetaTabular workspace.
meta-tabular deploy Create modeled objects on an Analysis Services tabular instance.
meta-tabular restore Promote a processed tabular database through backup and restore.
meta-tabular drop Drop a tabular database from an Analysis Services tabular instance.

meta-tabular --new-workspace Create an empty MetaTabular workspace. 1 option / 0 examples

Usage

meta-tabular --new-workspace <path>

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--new-workspace <path> Required. Directory where the empty MetaTabular workspace will be created. <path> True
meta-tabular deploy Create modeled objects on an Analysis Services tabular instance. 5 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-tabular deploy [--workspace <path>] --server <server> [--database-name <name>] [--drop-existing] [--no-process]

Guidance

Creates tabular database objects on an Analysis Services tabular instance.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--workspace <path> MetaTabular workspace to deploy. Defaults to the current directory. <path> False
--server <server> Required. Analysis Services tabular server. <server> True
--database-name <name> Optional target database name. Defaults to the modeled database name. <name> False
--drop-existing Drop an existing target database before create/deploy. False
--no-process Deploy metadata only and skip full processing. False
meta-tabular restore Promote a processed tabular database through backup and restore. 7 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-tabular restore --source-server <server> --source-database-name <name> --target-server <server> --target-database-name <name> --backup-file <path> [--drop-existing] [--overwrite-backup-file]

Guidance

Backs up a processed source tabular database and restores it as the target database.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--source-server <server> Required. Source Analysis Services server containing the processed database. <server> True
--source-database-name <name> Required. Source processed database name. <name> True
--target-server <server> Required. Target Analysis Services server. <server> True
--target-database-name <name> Required. Target database name to restore. <name> True
--backup-file <path> Required. Backup file path accessible to the Analysis Services service accounts. <path> True
--drop-existing Drop an existing target database before restore. False
--overwrite-backup-file Overwrite an existing backup file. False
meta-tabular drop Drop a tabular database from an Analysis Services tabular instance. 2 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-tabular drop --server <server> --database-name <name>

Guidance

Drops a tabular database from an Analysis Services tabular instance.

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--server <server> Required. Analysis Services tabular server. <server> True
--database-name <name> Required. Database name to drop. <name> True

Meta-BI / CLI

meta-transform-binding

`meta-transform-binding` binds MetaTransformScript workspaces to explicit source and target schema contracts, producing rowset, column, and validation evidence.

Commands

1 command exposed by meta-transform-binding.

CommandSummary
meta-transform-binding bind Bind all transform scripts and validate against source/target schema contracts into a new workspace.
meta-transform-binding bind Bind all transform scripts and validate against source/target schema contracts into a new workspace. 11 options / 4 examples

Usage

meta-transform-binding bind --transform-workspace <path> --source-schema <path> [--source-schema <path> ...] --target-schema <path> --execute-system <name> --new-workspace <path> [--execute-system-default-schema-name <schema>] [--ignore-target-columns <col[,col...]>] [--ignore-target-columns-if-present <col[,col...]>] [--data-type-conversion-workspace <path>] [--allow-partial] [--partial-report <path>]

Guidance

bind all transform scripts in a transform workspace into an explicit binding workspace (`rowsets`, `columns`, source/target SQL identifiers)

Options

OptionDescriptionValueRequired
--transform-workspace <path> Required. MetaTransformScript workspace to bind. <path> True
--source-schema <path> Required. Repeatable source MetaSchema workspace. <path> True
--target-schema <path> Required. Target MetaSchema workspace. <path> True
--execute-system <name> Required. Execution context for one/two-part source identifiers. <name> True
--new-workspace <path> Required. Directory where the binding workspace will be created. <path> True
--execute-system-default-schema-name <schema> Required when any one-part source identifier exists. <schema> True
--ignore-target-columns <col[,col...]> Optional comma-separated target columns to exclude from target conformance checks. <col[,col...]> False
--ignore-target-columns-if-present <col[,col...]> Optional comma-separated target columns to exclude only on target tables where they exist. <col[,col...]> False
--data-type-conversion-workspace <path> Optional sanctioned conversion policy workspace. Omitted uses built-in defaults. <path> False
--allow-partial Optional. Save only objects that bind and validate successfully; skipped objects are failures. False
--partial-report <path> Optional TSV report for objects skipped due to binding or validation failure. Requires --allow-partial. <path> False

Examples

meta-transform-binding bind --transform-workspace .\TransformWS --source-schema .\SourceSchemaWS --target-schema .\TargetSchemaWS --execute-system WarehouseDb --new-workspace .\BindingWS

meta-transform-binding bind --transform-workspace .\TransformWS --source-schema .\SalesSchemaWS --source-schema .\ReferenceSchemaWS --target-schema .\WarehouseSchemaWS --execute-system WarehouseDb --execute-system-default-schema-name dbo --new-workspace .\BindingWS --ignore-target-columns LoadUtc,RunId --ignore-target-columns-if-present UpdateAudit_ID --data-type-conversion-workspace .\MetaDataTypeConversion.Workspace

meta-transform-binding bind --transform-workspace .\TransformWS --source-schema .\SourceSchemaWS --target-schema .\TargetSchemaWS --execute-system WarehouseDb --execute-system-default-schema-name dbo --new-workspace .\BindingWS --allow-partial --partial-report .\binding-partial.tsv
meta-transform-binding bind --transform-workspace .\TransformWS --source-schema .\SourceSchemaWS --target-schema .\TargetSchemaWS --execute-system SalesDb --new-workspace .\BindingWS
meta-transform-binding bind --transform-workspace .\TransformWS --source-schema .\SalesSchemaWS --source-schema .\ReferenceSchemaWS --target-schema .\WarehouseSchemaWS --execute-system WarehouseDb --execute-system-default-schema-name dbo --new-workspace .\BindingWS --ignore-target-columns LoadUtc,RunId --ignore-target-columns-if-present UpdateAudit_ID
meta-transform-binding bind --transform-workspace .\TransformWS --source-schema .\SourceSchemaWS --target-schema .\TargetSchemaWS --execute-system SalesDb --execute-system-default-schema-name dbo --new-workspace .\BindingWS --allow-partial --partial-report .\binding-partial.tsv
Meta-BI / CLI

meta-transform-script

`meta-transform-script` models a bounded SQL transform surface as semantically round-trippable workspace data, preserving transform intent through import, emit, and diff cycles.

Commands

4 commands exposed by meta-transform-script.

CommandSummary
meta-transform-script from Import SQL file/code into a new or existing workspace.
meta-transform-script to Emit SQL files or SQL code from a MetaTransformScript workspace.
meta-transform-script stored-procedure View, add, and remove stored procedure contracts.
meta-transform-script target-identifiers Update TransformScript target identifiers.
meta-transform-script from Import SQL file/code into a new or existing workspace. 0 options / 4 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script from <source> [options]

Guidance

Sources: sql-file, sql-files, sql-code.

Examples

meta-transform-script from sql-file --path .\SourceViews\001_customer_order_summary\view.sql --target sales.CustomerOrderSummary --new-workspace .\TransformWorkspace
meta-transform-script from sql-file --path .\SourceViews\002_invoice_window\view.sql --target reporting.InvoiceWindow --workspace .\TransformWorkspace
meta-transform-script from sql-files --manifest .\import-manifest.tsv --new-workspace .\TransformWorkspace --report .\import-report.tsv --verbose
meta-transform-script from sql-code --code "select 1 as A" --name dbo.v_inline --target dbo.TargetTable --new-workspace .\TransformWorkspace
meta-transform-script to Emit SQL files or SQL code from a MetaTransformScript workspace. 0 options / 0 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script to <target> [options]

Guidance

Targets: sql-path, sql-code.

meta-transform-script stored-procedure View, add, and remove stored procedure contracts. 0 options / 3 examples

Usage

meta-transform-script stored-procedure <operation> [options]

Guidance

Operations: view-contract, add-contract, remove-contract.

Examples

meta-transform-script stored-procedure view-contract --workspace .\TransformWS
meta-transform-script stored-procedure add-contract --workspace .\TransformWS --name dq.RunReview --operation 10:read:src.Customer=CustomerInput --operation 20:reset:dq.CustomerReview --operation 30:append:dq.CustomerReview --operation 40:call:audit.MarkStarted
meta-transform-script stored-procedure remove-contract --workspace .\TransformWS --name dq.RunReview
meta-transform-script target-identifiers Update TransformScript target identifiers. 0 options / 1 example

Usage

meta-transform-script target-identifiers <operation> [options]

Guidance

Operations: from-pattern.

Examples

meta-transform-script target-identifiers from-pattern --workspace .\TransformWS --source-pattern "{schema}.{object}_TargetView" --target-pattern "KUBDW.{schema}.{object}"
